title: Claude CLI child process manager with stream-json I/O
purpose: Manages a single Claude CLI child process in headless mode, handling spawning, writing user messages, reading stdout/stderr, and process lifecycle management.
module_context: Exports ChildManager class and ChildExitInfo interface. Defines PERMISSION_MODE_MAP constant for permission mode translation. Uses EventEmitter for event handling.

steps.content: Check if the child process reference exists.
Verify the process has not exited.
Confirm the process has not been killed.
Construct command line arguments for the Claude CLI.
Include session ID and resume or session ID flags.
Set permission mode using the mapping table.
Add settings path if provided.
Include hook events and extra arguments if present.
Determine the Claude binary path or use default.
Resolve the spawn function or use default Bun.spawn.
Set environment variables including socket path if provided.
Spawn the Claude CLI process with constructed arguments.
Start reading stdout and stderr streams.
Listen for process exit to handle cleanup.
Verify the child process is alive before writing.
Format the user message as a JSON frame.
Write the formatted message to the child's stdin.
IF writing fails, emit an error event.
Check if the child process is alive before sending cancellation.
Clear any existing cancellation timer.
Send SIGINT signal to cancel the current turn.
Set a timer to send SIGTERM if the turn is not cancelled within 2 seconds.
Mark the manager as stopping to prevent further operations.
Clear any existing cancellation timer.
Verify the child process is alive before stopping.
End the stdin pipe to signal process termination.
Send SIGTERM signal to gracefully terminate the process.
Set a timer to forcefully kill the process if it doesn't terminate within 3 seconds.
Wait for the process to fully exit.
Initialize a stream reader for the provided input stream.
Create a text decoder for processing byte data.
Read data from the stream in a loop until done.
Accumulate data in a buffer until a newline is found.
Process complete lines from the buffer.
Emit parsed stdout frames or stderr lines.
Handle any stream reading errors gracefully.
Attempt to parse the stdout line as JSON.
IF parsing succeeds, emit the parsed frame.
IF parsing fails, increment the parse error streak.
IF three consecutive parse errors occur, emit a fatal error and kill the process.
ELSE emit a recoverable parse error.
Extract the signal code from the process.
Emit an exit event with the process code and signal.
